content.text:
```json
{
  "module": "scheduling",
  "scores": {
    "visual_polish": 28,
    "domain_depth": 25,
    "data_richness": 18,
    "interaction_quality": 20,
    "ai_integration": 8,
    "mobile_first": 12,
    "performance": 30,
    "typescript_quality": 35,
    "error_handling": 22,
    "realtime": 20,
    "accessibility": 10,
    "security": 28,
    "differentiation": 12,
    "enterprise_readiness": 15
  },
  "overall_score": 20,
  "issues": [
    {
      "id": "scheduling-C4-001",
      "severity": "critical",
      "dimension": "domain_depth",
      "title": "No P6/MS Project schedule import capability exists",
      "file": "src/pages/Schedule.tsx",
      "description": "PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA §6 criterion 11 requires 'Import from P6/MS Project' CTA. PRODUCTION_ROADMAP P0-2 defines schedule import as a dealbreaker. No import button, no file parser, no upload flow exists. Superintendents cannot see their P6 schedule in SiteSync, which kills adoption.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/pages/Schedule.tsx, in the default export Schedule component: Add a 'Import Schedule' button in the page header area next to any existing action buttons. BEFORE: The page header has no import functionality. AFTER: A secondary button styled with `theme.colors.border` border and `theme.colors.text` text reads 'Import Schedule' with an Upload icon from lucide-react. Clicking it opens a modal (use a useState boolean `showImportModal`) that renders a new `<ScheduleImportModal open={showImportModal} onClose={() => setShowImportModal(false)} onImportComplete={() => { setShowImportModal(false); /* refetch schedule data */ }} />` component. For now, create the modal inline in this file as a simple dialog with: (1) a drag-drop zone accepting .xer, .xml, .mpp files using an `<input type='file' accept='.xer,.xml,.mpp' />`, (2) a file name display after selection, (3) a 'Preview & Import' button that is disabled until a file is selected, (4) a 'Cancel' button. The modal should use inline styles with theme tokens (background: theme.colors.surface, borderRadius: theme.radius.lg, padding: theme.spacing.xl). Include proper ARIA: role='dialog', aria-modal='true', aria-labelledby pointing to the modal title. The actual parsing logic will be added in a subsequent prompt via an edge function. For now, clicking 'Preview & Import' should show a toast 'Schedule import coming soon' using any existing toast utility in the codebase. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "scheduling-C4-002",
      "severity": "critical",
      "dimension": "data_richness",
      "title": "Schedule page shows no baseline vs actual comparison",
      "file": "src/pages/Schedule.tsx",
      "description": "PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA §6 criterion 8 requires 'Baseline comparison: toggle to show original baseline dates as ghost bars behind current bars.' The Gantt chart has no baseline rendering. The `schedule_phases` table has `baseline_start` and `baseline_end` columns but they are never visualized. Without baseline comparison, PMs cannot assess schedule variance visually.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/pages/Schedule.tsx, find the Gantt chart rendering section (where schedule phase bars are drawn). Add a toggle button labeled 'Show Baseline' in the schedule toolbar/header area using a useState boolean `showBaseline` (default false). BEFORE: Only current schedule bars are rendered with no baseline reference. AFTER: When `showBaseline` is true, for each schedule phase that has non-null `baseline_start` and `baseline_end` values, render a semi-transparent gray bar (background: 'rgba(156, 163, 175, 0.3)', border: '1px dashed #9CA3AF') positioned behind the actual schedule bar. The baseline bar should use the same horizontal scale/positioning logic as the actual bar but reference `baseline_start` and `baseline_end` instead of `start_date` and `end_date`. Add a legend entry: a small gray dashed rectangle labeled 'Baseline' next to the toggle. The toggle button should use theme tokens: when active, background is theme.colors.primary with white text; when inactive, outlined style. If no phases have baseline data, the toggle should be disabled with a tooltip 'No baseline dates available'. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "scheduling-C4-003",
      "severity": "critical",
      "dimension": "interaction_quality",
      "title": "Critical path not visually highlighted on Gantt chart",
      "file": "src/pages/Schedule.tsx",
      "description": "PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA §6 criteria 1 and 2 require critical path activities highlighted in red. The `is_critical_path` field exists in the data model and `calculateCriticalPath` exists in `src/lib/criticalPath.ts`, but the Gantt bars do not use red coloring for critical path items. Critical path visibility is the single most important schedule feature for PMs.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/pages/Schedule.tsx, find where Gantt chart bars are rendered for each schedule phase/activity (likely a map over activities rendering div elements with colored backgrounds). BEFORE: All bars use the same color scheme regardless of critical path status. AFTER: For any activity where `is_critical_path === true` OR where `float_days === 0`, apply these visual changes: (1) The bar background becomes '#EF4444' (red-500) instead of the default color, (2) Add a subtle red glow: boxShadow '0 0 8px rgba(239, 68, 68, 0.3)', (3) On hover, show a tooltip or title attribute that says 'Critical Path — 0 days float'. For non-critical activities, keep existing colors but also show float days on hover: 'Float: X days'. Add a small legend item in the chart header: a red rectangle labeled 'Critical Path'. Also add a filter toggle button 'Critical Path Only' that, when active, filters the displayed activities to only show those where `is_critical_path === true`. Use a useState boolean `showCriticalOnly`. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "scheduling-C4-004",
      "severity": "major",
      "dimension": "visual_polish",
      "title": "Schedule page missing KPI metric cards at top",
      "file": "src/pages/Schedule.tsx",
      "description": "PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA §6 specifies KPI Cards: Schedule Variance (days), Critical Path Items, On Track %, Complete %. Design Standards require every page to start with 3-5 metric cards. The schedule page lacks these summary metrics, making it impossible for a PM to assess schedule health at a glance.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/pages/Schedule.tsx, at the top of the page content (after the page title/header but before the Gantt chart or activity list), add a metrics row. BEFORE: The page jumps directly to the chart/table with no summary metrics. AFTER: Add a grid of 4 metric cards using the existing MetricBox or Card primitive from `src/components/Primitives.tsx` (or create inline styled cards matching the design system: white background, 1px border #E5E7EB, borderRadius 12px, padding 24px). Calculate these metrics from the schedule activities data: (1) 'Schedule Variance' — compute as the difference in days between the latest activity's `end_date` and its `baseline_end`, show as '+X days' (red if positive meaning late) or 'On Time' (green), icon Calendar from lucide-react. (2) 'Critical Path Items' — count of activities where `is_critical_path === true`, colored red if > 0, icon AlertTriangle. (3) 'On Track' — percentage of non-completed activities where `end_date` <= `baseline_end` or where no delay exists, show as 'XX%' with green/amber/red coloring (green >= 80%, amber >= 60%, red < 60%), icon TrendingUp. (4) 'Complete' — average `percent_complete` across all activities, show as 'XX%' with a small progress bar, icon CheckCircle. Use the grid style: display grid, gridTemplateColumns 'repeat(4, 1fr)', gap theme.spacing.md, marginBottom theme.spacing.lg. Handle empty data: if no activities, show '—' for each value. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "scheduling-C4-005",
      "severity": "major",
      "dimension": "enterprise_readiness",
      "title": "No empty state with onboarding guidance when schedule has no data",
      "file": "src/pages/Schedule.tsx",
      "description": "PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA §6 criterion 11 requires: 'Build your schedule to track every phase from mobilization to closeout. CTA: Create First Phase and Import from P6/MS Project.' Currently if the schedule has no activities, users see either a blank area or a generic message. A helpful empty state with dual CTAs is required to guide new users.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/pages/Schedule.tsx, find the section that renders the Gantt chart or activity list. Add a conditional check: when the schedule activities array is empty (length === 0) AND not loading, render a designed empty state instead of the chart. BEFORE: Empty schedule shows blank space or generic 'no data' text. AFTER: Render a centered container (maxWidth 480px, margin '80px auto', textAlign 'center') with: (1) A Calendar icon from lucide-react at 64px size, color theme.colors.textMuted, marginBottom 24px. (2) Heading text 'Build Your Project Schedule' in 20px font-weight 600 color theme.colors.text. (3) Body text 'Track every phase from mobilization to closeout. Import your existing P6 or MS Project schedule, or build from scratch.' in 14px color theme.colors.textMuted, marginBottom 32px. (4) Two buttons side by side in a flex row with gap 12px: Primary button 'Import Schedule' (background theme.colors.primary, color white, onClick opens the import modal from scheduling-C4-001) and Secondary button 'Create First Phase' (background white, border 1px solid theme.colors.border, color theme.colors.text). Do NOT use hyphens in any text. Use 'from scratch' not 'from-scratch'. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    }
  ],
  "invented_features": [
    {
      "title": "Weather Overlay on Schedule",
      "description": "Overlay 7-day weather forecast directly on the Gantt timeline. Flag outdoor activities (concrete pours, roofing, excavation) that overlap with rain/snow/extreme heat days. Show a weather risk indicator per activity: green (clear), amber (marginal), red (likely delay). Uses existing weather_cache data joined against schedule_phases where outdoor_activity is true. This feature does not exist in Procore, Fieldwire, or any competing PM tool.",
      "priority": "P1",
      "effort": "Medium (2 prompts: weather data join + Gantt overlay rendering)"
    }
  ],
  "competitive_intel": "Procore's schedule module is view-only with no critical path calculation, no baseline overlay, and no weather integration. They rely on users importing from P6 and viewing a static Gantt. SiteSync's opportunity is a living, intelligent schedule that predicts delays and highlights risk — something no construction PM tool does natively today. Fieldwire has zero schedule capability. Buildertrend has basic Gantt but no CPM or P6 import. The P0-2 schedule import feature is the single highest-impact differentiator for superintendent adoption.",
  "summary": "The scheduling module scores 20/100 — critically underbuilt for a construction PM platform where the schedule is checked daily by every superintendent. The Gantt chart exists structurally but lacks the four features that make it useful: P6/MS Project import (P0 dealbreaker), baseline vs actual comparison (required by acceptance criteria), critical path highlighting (the most important visual signal), and KPI summary cards (design standard for every page). The data model in `schedule_phases` and the critical path algorithm in `src/lib/criticalPath.ts` provide a solid foundation, but the frontend fails to surface this data meaningfully. The five prompts target: (1) import modal scaffold for P6/.xer files, (2) baseline ghost bars behind actual bars, (3) red critical path highlighting with filter toggle, (4) four KPI metric cards computing schedule variance/critical items/on-track%/completion%, and (5) a guided empty state with dual CTAs. These changes would bring the module from unusable to functional, roughly doubling the score to 40-45 range and unblocking the P0-2 roadmap item."
}
```
